name: CQC inspection evidence assembly
description: Automatic evidence compilation against quality statements, weeks to hours
current_process: When a CQC inspection is announced (typically with two weeks' notice, or sometimes unannounced), care home managers scramble to compile an evidence portfolio demonstrating compliance across all five key questions (Safe, Effective, Caring, Responsive, Well-Led) and their associated quality statements. This involves: manually searching through filing cabinets and multiple digital systems for policies, audit reports, training records, incident logs, complaints records, safeguarding referrals, staff supervision notes, resident feedback, and meeting minutes. Evidence is organised into physical or digital folders mapped to each quality statement. Managers cross-reference each quality statement against available evidence, identifying and filling gaps. The CQC requests specific information with five working days to respond. Many managers describe this as weeks of intensive preparation — reviewing documentation, updating overdue audits, and assembling a coherent narrative of quality and governance. Between inspections, evidence is rarely organised systematically, leading to a 'panic and scramble' cycle.
time_spent: Evidence assembly for a CQC inspection typically consumes 2–4 weeks of management time when done reactively. Registered managers report spending full days (8+ hours) assembling evidence portfolios, with some booking out entire days for mock inspections. Ongoing evidence maintenance (keeping folders updated between inspections) requires an estimated 2–5 hours per week. The CQC inspection itself lasts 2–5 hours on-site, but preparation dwarfs this. Under the new Single Assessment Framework, CQC increasingly monitors services continuously using real-time data, making ongoing evidence readiness even more critical.
ai_solution: AI can automate the compilation and organisation of CQC evidence by: (1) Continuous evidence mapping — automatically tagging and filing every document, audit, incident report, training record, and feedback entry against the relevant CQC quality statement as it is created, eliminating retrospective assembly. (2) Gap analysis — continuously scanning across all quality statements to identify missing or outdated evidence (e.g., expired training certificates, overdue audits, lapsed policies) and alerting managers. (3) Evidence narrative generation — auto-generating summary reports for each quality statement that describe what evidence exists, what it demonstrates, and what actions have been taken. (4) Inspection readiness scoring — providing a real-time 'inspection readiness' dashboard showing compliance status across all five key questions. (5) Document retrieval — instant search and retrieval of any piece of evidence by quality statement, date range, or keyword, replacing manual file searches. Quik AI's Carey can compile CQC evidence as part of its audit functionality. EverythingCQC offers quality automation systems for inspection readiness.
buyer_language: "Every time CQC comes, we spend weeks pulling everything together — digging through files, chasing missing documents, updating things we should have done months ago. It's stressful, it takes me away from the home, and I'm never confident we haven't missed something. I need everything to be ready all the time, not just when we're told they're coming."
outcome_statement: CQC evidence portfolio assembled automatically and continuously — inspection preparation reduced from weeks of scrambling to hours of review, with real-time readiness scoring across all quality statements.
regulatory_alignment: Directly supports compliance with all five CQC key questions (Safe, Effective, Caring, Responsive, Well-Led) and the Single Assessment Framework's quality statements. Under the new framework, CQC no longer scores at evidence category level but uses professional judgement across all evidence for each quality statement, making comprehensive evidence organisation essential. Aligns with the CQC's shift toward continuous monitoring using real-time data and feedback. The 10 Year Health Plan includes an AI-powered early warning system that will use provider data, further increasing the importance of well-organised digital evidence.
existing_tech_gap: Current compliance tools (Radar Healthcare, GoAudits, Care Audit Tool, SafeWork) offer digital audit templates, action tracking, and some evidence storage, but require manual organisation of evidence against quality statements. KareInn provides compliance dashboards and auto-populates some incident data into Radar Healthcare. EverythingCQC offers quality automation but is not AI-powered in the generative sense. No current platform uses AI to automatically tag incoming documents to quality statements, generate evidence narratives, or provide intelligent gap analysis across the full CQC framework. AI adds unique value through automated classification, continuous gap detection, and narrative generation.
implementation_complexity: Requires integration. AI evidence assembly needs access to multiple data sources within the care home: care planning software, HR/training systems, incident reporting tools, policy management systems, feedback platforms, and meeting minute records. Integration with existing systems via API is essential for automated document tagging. Some elements (e.g., readiness dashboards, gap analysis) could be delivered as a standalone overlay with manual document upload as an interim step.
proof_of_concept_availability: Partial. Quik AI's Carey offers CQC evidence compilation as part of its broader audit toolset, with named case studies (Wavemill, Rose Care Group). EverythingCQC provides quality automation systems. Radar Healthcare and KareInn offer CQC dashboards with some automation. However, no fully AI-powered, end-to-end evidence assembly product purpose-built for the CQC Single Assessment Framework exists in the UK market yet — making this a significant opportunity for a bespoke AI solution.
